pi_items.source: Cloned NREL/SAM repository tag 2024.12.12.r0.ssc.298 (corresponding to SAM version installed by install_sam.sh) and analyzed deploy/libraries/CEC Modules.csv. 'Canadian Solar Inc.' was renamed to 'CSI Solar Co. Ltd.', making the expected verifier string missing.
Analyzed deploy/libraries/CEC Inverters.csv from NREL/SAM repository tag 2024.12.12.r0.ssc.298 and confirmed 'SMA America' components exist in the 10kW to 20kWac range.
verifier.py uses expected_inverter_count = 4 as default and strictly checks inv_count == 4.
verifier.py defaults expected_dc_capacity_min to 45.0 and strictly asserts expected_dc_capacity_min <= dc_cap.
verifier.py defaults expected_dc_capacity_max to 55.0 and strictly asserts dc_cap <= expected_dc_capacity_max.

privileged_info_summary: The environment's installer (install_sam.sh) sets up NREL SAM version 2024-12-12. By inspecting the real-world CEC Module and Inverter databases corresponding to that release in the NREL/SAM GitHub repository, the 'SMA' inverter brand correctly exists. However, the 'Canadian Solar' module brand expected by the prompt and verifier has been updated to 'CSI Solar Co. Ltd.' in the real CEC database. This creates a critical defect that makes the task impossible to natively pass using the CEC Database.
